title: CSS for Modern Web Applications Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties and tools for creating robust and cross-browser web experiences with modern Web Apps.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application in CSS for SPAs (Single-Page Applications)?
options: Table Layouts
Float Layouts
Using CSS Modules or Utility-First Frameworks (Tailwind CSS) for component-level styles.
Absolute Positioning only

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using CSS Modules in a React or Vue project?
options: It makes your CSS files smaller and more readable.
It 'scopes' your CSS to only one specific component, preventing classes from accidentally clashing with others.
It is always supported by every browser.
It replaces the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: What is 'CSS-in-JS' inside a modern React application?
options: To make the site load much faster.
Using JavaScript to write your CSS rules directly within your component files (e.g., Styled Components).
To choose the colors for the animation.
To automatically generate CSS animations.

questions.question: Why is it important to almost ALWAYS avoid 'Universal Selectors' in modern Component-based CSS?
options: They are difficult to maintain.
They can break the isolation and style encapsulation of your individual components.
They are not supported in modern browsers.
They take up no space in the browser.

questions.question: Which of these is a best practice for a professional-style stylesheet for modern web apps?
options: Only using inline styles.
Using a global style file for everything.
Using 'Code Splitting' to only load the CSS needed for the current route on the page.
Using variables for colors and spacing.
